summary: Tested ScubaPlaydate CMS backend (11/11 pytest passed) and frontend (login, dashboard, articles, users, banners, settings, homepage, category pages all load). Critical TinyMCE blocker found: editor is in read-only mode due to missing API key.

minor.issue: POST returns 200 instead of 201 for resource creation (consistent across CRUD endpoints)
Any authenticated user (writer/editor) can create/edit/delete ANY article — no ownership checks; role-based authorization is missing for content endpoints
No file type/size validation; accepts any file extension, security risk for arbitrary uploads
Tracking endpoints have no auth and no verification banner exists; silently succeed on invalid IDs

ui_bugs.issue: TinyMCE is using 'no-api-key' from CDN; editor is in READ-ONLY mode and shows a 'Finish setting up / Add your API key' overlay covering the content area. Article content cannot be authored. Console errors: 'All created TinyMCE editors are configured to be read-only' and 'editor is disabled because the API key could not be validated'

integration_issues.issue: Due to TinyMCE read-only mode, content_html cannot be entered through the UI even though backend POST /api/articles works fine via API

issues: Footer uses dark navy background which conflicts with the requested 'sleek clean white background' design archetype — verify intent
Empty state 'No articles found' is shown because no seeded articles exist; consider seed data for demos

action_items: CRITICAL: Replace TinyMCE 'no-api-key' with a valid API key (TINYMCE_API_KEY env var) OR self-host TinyMCE community edition. Without this, no articles can be authored via the CMS UI.
Add role-based authorization to article create/update/delete endpoints (currently any authenticated user, including writers, can modify any article).
Add file type/size validation in /api/upload (whitelist: jpg/png/webp/gif; max ~5MB).
Consider returning 201 for POST creation endpoints instead of 200.
Add basic seed articles so public homepage and category pages show real content.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: /api/articles POST/PUT/DELETE only requires get_current_user — writers can edit any article. Add ownership or role gate (admin/editor only for delete).
server.py: /api/upload accepts arbitrary file extensions; vulnerable to upload of executables/SVG XSS. Validate content_type and extension.
server.py: /api/banners/{id}/click and /impression are public AND don't verify the banner exists — easy to spam metrics. Add existence check at minimum.
server.py: Uses deprecated @app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown'); migrate to FastAPI lifespan handlers.
Frontend: TinyMCE integration depends on Tiny Cloud CDN with 'no-api-key'; needs configurable API key via env (REACT_APP_TINYMCE_API_KEY) or switch to self-hosted.
seed_data_creation: None. Tests clean up TEST_ prefixed data. Public site shows empty states because no demo articles exist.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py covers auth, users, articles (CRUD + slug + filters), banners (CRUD + tracking), settings, upload, and role-based access. Re-run with: pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v. The blocking issue for frontend article authoring is TinyMCE no-api-key read-only mode — verify after main agent fixes the API key. Public homepage/category pages render correctly but show 'No articles found' empty state.
rca_of_the_issue: TinyMCE Cloud CDN script (cdn.tiny.cloud/1/no-api-key/...) requires a valid API key; the license validation server returns 404 for 'no-api-key' which forces all editors into read-only mode. Fix: sign up for a free Tiny Cloud key and pass it via apiKey prop on the <Editor /> component, sourced from process.env.REACT_APP_TINYMCE_API_KEY, OR use the self-hosted tinymce npm package with no apiKey requirement.
